content: Hi. I want to use a static variable in my driver, which will be accessed by different threads. However, it is hard to wrap this variable with a lock. Because I can't use lazy_static! and SyncLazy in rust-for-linux. I have to use a "mut static" variable and unsafe blocks for now. Meanwhile, there is no protection for the read or write to this variable. I wonder the reason for not importing something like SyncLazy into rust-for-linux. If SyncLazy should be imported, can I open a PR for importing it into rust-for-linux? Thanks for your help!
This is exactly what I want. I don't notice this sample due to the patchset version I use. I will try to move my project into the mainline version. Thanks for your help!
Hongyu Li has marked this topic as resolved.
